themes.label: Home / Belonging
themes.description: Dreams of a safe home base and reciprocal belonging with family, a partner, friends, or community.
themes.classification_guidance: Use when sanctuary, rootedness, reciprocal love, family, friendship, or community is itself a desired outcome. Owning a house or land without relational or rooted language is not enough.

themes.label: Cultivation
themes.description: Dreams of growing or tending land, food, a creative practice, study, a business, or a way of life over time.
themes.classification_guidance: Use when making, growing, learning, practicing, building, or tending is itself a desired ongoing practice. A creative job, business, homestead, or piece of land does not establish Cultivation unless the process matters explicitly.

themes.label: Service
themes.description: Dreams of directly helping, healing, teaching, advocating for, or materially supporting people, communities, or animals.
themes.classification_guidance: Use when direct help, care, repair, teaching for recipient wellbeing or access, advocacy, or material support is a stated purpose. Do not infer Service from a helping profession, beneficiary, or caring personality when the help is not part of the aspiration. Teaching whose stated end is carrying knowledge or skill outward is Transmission instead.

themes.label: Freedom
themes.description: Dreams of autonomy over movement, time, place, identity, or the terms of work and daily life.
themes.classification_guidance: Use when mobility, travel, release, independence, time autonomy, or authentic self-direction is itself desired. Money or stability that merely enables another outcome is not automatically Freedom.

themes.label: Stewardship
themes.description: Dreams of taking durable responsibility for land, resources, institutions, family wellbeing, or community continuity.
themes.classification_guidance: Use when durable responsibility beyond the self is explicit: caring for land, resources, an institution, family wellbeing, or community continuity over time. The presence of land, animals, or children alone is not enough.

themes.label: Self-Sufficiency
themes.description: Dreams of a dependable material base—income, health, home, land, or food—that reduces survival pressure and reliance on unstable systems.
themes.classification_guidance: Use when financial security, stable work, health, housing, land, food, or another dependable material foundation is itself desired. Do not assign it when money or property appears only as a means to another stated outcome.

themes.label: Transmission
themes.description: Dreams of carrying knowledge, art, story, skill, culture, or legacy outward to others or forward to another generation.
themes.classification_guidance: Use when an audience or recipient is explicit through teaching that carries knowledge or skill outward, publishing, performing, sharing, communicating, influencing, or carrying a legacy. Private making, study, or research alone is Cultivation rather than Transmission; teaching centered on recipient care, access, or wellbeing is Service instead.

roles.description: The single clearest end-state at the center of the response.
A separate stand-alone aspiration of comparable emphasis, supported by its own clause.
A means, setting, beneficiary, consequence, or weaker signal that helps explain a public theme but is not independently dominant.

score_dimensions.description: The theme is stated in the verbatim response rather than inferred.

anchors.0: No direct support; the theme would have to be inferred from context or a noun alone.
anchors.1: The response suggests the theme, but the desired outcome is ambiguous or indirect.
anchors.2: An exact verbatim clause directly establishes the theme as something the respondent wants.

score_dimensions.description: The theme is central to what the respondent wants, not incidental context.

anchors.0: Incidental context, biography, or a detail outside the stated dream.
anchors.1: Relevant to the dream but secondary to a clearer desired outcome.
anchors.2: A central end-state the respondent explicitly foregrounds or independently enumerates.

score_dimensions.description: The aspiration still establishes this theme if the response's other dream clauses are removed.

anchors.0: The signal disappears when its enabling or surrounding clause is removed.
anchors.1: The signal is meaningful but depends partly on another outcome, setting, or beneficiary.
anchors.2: Its own exact evidence clause remains a complete, independently meaningful aspiration.

score_dimensions.description: This theme explains the evidence better than an adjacent theme does.

anchors.0: Another theme clearly explains the same evidence better.
anchors.1: The evidence sits on a documented boundary and either theme remains plausible.
anchors.2: The wording distinctly matches this theme and not a documented adjacent theme.

confidence_policy.rule: The public role qualifies, has a total score of 8 for primary or at least 7 for co-dominant, and has no unresolved boundary ambiguity.
The public role qualifies but uses the minimum score, or one documented boundary requires judgment.
The candidate remains provisional because evidence, centrality, standalone status, or specificity is uncertain; a low-confidence candidate cannot be published without explicit adjudication.

shared_boundaries: Classify the verbatim response text; the Dreams summary may help navigation but cannot override the source text.

A candidate must be a current first-person aspiration. Do not classify hardship, biography, present occupation, astrology, lessons, people attracted, another person's goal, a negated goal, or a past or abandoned dream.
A public theme must describe a dominant desired outcome, not merely a mechanism, setting, beneficiary, consequence, or inferred facet.
Choose exactly one primary theme when any candidate scores at least 7. Leave the response publicly unthemed when none reaches 7.

When eligible primary candidates tie, choose the theme with the earliest separate evidence clause in the verbatim response. If the same clause supports both, prefer the more specific theme; if that also ties, use taxonomy order. A remaining eligible tied outcome may be co-dominant.
Add a co-dominant theme only when it has a separate evidence clause, scores at least 6, and is within one point of the primary score.
A candidate scoring 6 or more may remain supporting when it is a means, setting, beneficiary, consequence, or lacks a separate stand-alone clause; score alone never makes a theme public.
Publish no more than two themes by default. A third requires three separately stated aspirations, a score of at least 7 for each, and explicit adjudication. Never publish four or more themes.
One noun cannot inflate into several themes. A house, homestead, farm, job, business, creative practice, family, or trip establishes only what the surrounding clause explicitly makes important.
Prefer the desired end over the means used to obtain it. Money so the respondent can travel supports Freedom unless material security is also an independent desired end.
Money, wealth, fame, luxury, recognition, and status do not establish Self-Sufficiency unless the response explicitly connects them to security, needs, stability, or reduced dependence.
Bare Freedom is specific enough for Freedom. Bare stability, security, success, impact, love, or legacy requires a material, relational, or action domain before it can establish a theme.

A single teaching or education clause cannot count as both Service and Transmission. Choose Service when the desired end is recipient care, access, healing, advocacy, or wellbeing; choose Transmission when the desired end is carrying knowledge, art, story, culture, or skill outward. Only separately stated ends may support both themes.

Distinguish Stewardship from Transmission by what continues. Stewardship maintains responsibility for land, resources, an institution, family wellbeing, or community infrastructure; Transmission passes knowledge, art, story, culture, skill, or a named legacy onward. Asking others to continue the respondent's dream or legacy is Transmission unless the respondent separately takes durable responsibility for the institution, resource, family, or community involved.
An exact excerpt from the verbatim response is required for every primary, co-dominant, and supporting classification.
Record plausible adjacent themes that were rejected and state whether they were merely inferred, a means, a setting, a beneficiary, a consequence, or better explained by another theme.
Peace, rest, recovery, recognition, status, happiness, and literal sleep dreams remain publicly unthemed unless a surrounding aspiration independently establishes one of the seven themes.
